entities.name: Freelancer
entities.note: Primary user of the system; logs hours and exports invoices.
entities.source: freelancers can log their billable hours

entities.name: Client Project
entities.note: A named project owned by a client, against which a freelancer logs billable hours.
entities.source: log their billable hours against client projects

entities.name: Time Entry
entities.note: A single logged unit of billable work (hours) tied to a project.

entities.name: Invoice
entities.note: A monthly PDF document summarising billable hours for a client project.

entities.name: Currency
entities.note: A currency designation applied to monetary amounts (rates, totals) in the system.

entities.name: Client
entities.note: The party that owns a project and to whom an invoice is addressed. Named indirectly via 'client projects' and the invoicing context.
entities.rationale: Invoicing is addressed to a billable party. 'Client projects' presupposes a client record to associate projects and invoice recipients.
entities.source: client projects … export a monthly PDF invoice

entities.name: Billable Rate
entities.note: A monetary rate (per hour) used to compute invoice totals from logged hours.
entities.rationale: 'Billable hours' on an invoice must produce a monetary amount; a rate is the only mechanism to convert hours to money.
entities.source: billable hours … export a monthly PDF invoice … multiple currencies

explicit_requirements.text: The system must be delivered as a web application.
A freelancer must be able to log billable hours against a client project.
explicit_requirements.source: freelancers can log their billable hours against client projects

explicit_requirements.text: A freelancer must be able to export a monthly PDF invoice.
The system must support multiple currencies.

implied_requirements.text: A freelancer must be able to create and authenticate an account.
implied_requirements.source: freelancers can log their billable hours
implied_requirements.rationale: Logging hours is a user-owned action; the system must distinguish one freelancer's data from another's, requiring identity and authentication.

implied_requirements.text: A freelancer must be able to create and manage client projects within the system.
implied_requirements.source: log their billable hours against client projects
implied_requirements.rationale: Hours cannot be logged 'against' a project that does not yet exist in the system. Project creation is a prerequisite.

implied_requirements.text: All logged time entries must be persisted and associated with their project and freelancer.
implied_requirements.source: log their billable hours … export a monthly PDF invoice
implied_requirements.rationale: The invoice aggregates hours over a month. Hours must be stored and queryable by period to generate the invoice.

implied_requirements.text: The generated PDF invoice must aggregate the month's time entries into line items showing hours, rate, and total.
implied_requirements.rationale: A PDF invoice for billable hours is not meaningful without itemised hours and resulting monetary amounts. This is the minimum content a recipient would require to verify the bill.

implied_requirements.text: Each monetary value in the system (rate, line-item total, invoice total) must be stored and displayed with its associated currency.
implied_requirements.rationale: Multi-currency support requires every monetary amount to carry a currency designation; otherwise amounts from different currencies are indistinguishable.

implied_requirements.text: A billable hourly rate must be configurable per project or per time entry to compute invoice monetary totals.
implied_requirements.source: billable hours … export a monthly PDF invoice … multiple currencies
implied_requirements.rationale: 'Billable hours' on an invoice must produce a monetary total. A rate is the only mechanism to convert hours to currency amounts. Without it no invoice total can be calculated.

stated_constraints.text: The product must be a web application.
The system should be live within approximately two months.
stated_constraints.source: Ideally live in a couple of months

stated_constraints.text: This is a net-new build with no existing system or codebase.
stated_constraints.source: No existing system — we're starting from scratch

unknowns.text: Which specific currencies must be supported, and at what granularity is currency set (per-freelancer, per-client, per-project, or per-time-entry)?
What authentication mechanism is required (email + password, OAuth/social login, SSO)?
unknowns.source: freelancers can log their billable hours

unknowns.text: Is a 'client' a data record only visible to the freelancer, or does the client also have a login and portal?
unknowns.source: client projects … export a monthly PDF invoice

unknowns.text: Can one freelancer account manage multiple clients and projects, and is there a limit?
unknowns.source: log their billable hours against client projects

unknowns.text: What are the PDF invoice content and formatting requirements (logo, legal fields, payment terms, numbering scheme, template)?
Does 'monthly' invoice mean a fixed calendar month, or is the billing period configurable (e.g. any date range)?
Where is the billable rate configured — per project, per client, or per individual time entry — and can it vary over time?
unknowns.source: billable hours … export a monthly PDF invoice

unknowns.text: Does multi-currency require live exchange-rate conversion between currencies, or is each project/invoice fixed to a single currency?
What is the expected user (freelancer) volume and concurrent usage scale?
Are there hosting, cloud provider, or infrastructure preferences or constraints?
unknowns.source: No existing system — we're starting from scratch

unknowns.text: Are there team or agency accounts (multiple freelancers under one organisation), or is the system strictly single-freelancer per account?
unknowns.source: freelancers can log their billable hours

unknowns.text: Are there compliance or data-residency requirements (GDPR, financial record retention, etc.)?
unknowns.source: export a monthly PDF invoice … multiple currencies
